post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)
Diff contains only an execution evidence result.json file. No source code changes to execute_bead_review.go or any tests. Acceptance criteria 1-4 cannot be evaluated as implemented.
result_rev=6dc6d11357bd7916cd466387f3831621d0cd6f76
base_rev=1b1c578b27c46ee2a6f3b90bfce799e221b3b69e

Bead ddx-2436fa73 — no code changes required this attempt.

Status: All acceptance criteria are already satisfied by commit 0dd8a817
("fix(review): R4 pairing — drop impl-harness fallback, emit pairing-degraded
[ddx-2436fa73]"), which is an ancestor of this worktree's base
(10331745 chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260502T135553-29fca310).

The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note on this bead was a false positive: the reviewer
saw a merge whose diff contained only a result.json artifact, but the actual
source changes had already landed in commit 0dd8a817 against
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go and 17 sibling files
(including a 213-line execute_bead_review_pairing_test.go).

AC ↔ evidence mapping (all in cli/internal/agent/):

AC1 — execute_bead_review.go no longer defaults to implementer harness.
  Evidence: execute_bead_review.go:600-608 — when r.Harness is empty the
  reviewer falls through to the literal "claude", explicitly NOT to
  impl.Harness; comment at 601-604 documents the R4 rationale.
  Test: TestReviewBead_NoLongerDefaultsToImplementerHarness — PASS.

AC2 — Reviewer call carries Role='reviewer' + same CorrelationID +
       MinPower = impl.ActualPower + 1.
  Evidence: BuildReviewExecuteRequest at execute_bead_review.go:474-502
  copies impl.Correlation, overlays role=reviewer, and sets
  MinPowerOverride = impl.ActualPower + 1.
  Tests: TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest,
         TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest_ZeroPowerLeavesMinPowerZero — PASS.

AC3 — review-pairing-degraded event emitted when reviewer's
       RoutingActual.Provider == implementer's.
  Evidence: ReviewBead at execute_bead_review.go:744-752 appends a
  bead.BeadEvent with Kind=ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind
  ("review-pairing-degraded", declared at line 29) when
  actualProvider == impl.Provider.
  Test: TestReviewBead_DegradedPath_SameProvider_EmitsEvent — PASS.

AC4 — Tests cover happy / degraded / event payload with both routing
       details.
  Evidence: execute_bead_review_pairing_test.go provides
  TestReviewBead_HappyPath_DifferentProvider_NoDegradedEvent (no event
  emitted), TestReviewBead_DegradedPath_SameProvider_EmitsEvent (event
  emitted, body asserted to contain impl_* and reviewer_* fields via
  reviewPairingDegradedBody at execute_bead_review.go:507-519), and
  TestReviewBead_NoLongerDefaultsToImplementerHarness. All PASS.

Verification run (this worktree):
  go test ./internal/agent/ -run "ReviewBead|BuildReview|PairingDegraded|ReviewPairing" -count=1
  -> 13/13 PASS, ok.

Conclusion: nothing to implement. The reopen was driven by a stale review
of a merge that landed evidence-only after the actual fix; the source-of-
truth code in HEAD already meets the contract. No commit produced this
attempt; this rationale is the evidence artifact.
